title: Brathys mutisiana
intro: Brathys mutisiana, also known as Mutisia, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Brathys mutisiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Brathys mutisiana is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a tincture, to leverage its health benefits and treat various ailments.
